blocks.text: DTC P2101: Abnormal throttle actuator characteristics

The DBW system opens and closes the throttle valve under electronic control. The system consists of a throttle actuator built into the throttle body, throttle valve and throttle position sensor 1/2, accelerator pedal position sensor 1/2, DBW relay, and PGM-FI ECU. When the accelerator pedal is operated, the accelerator pedal position sensor is driven to detect the amount of depressing the accelerator pedal. The throttle valve opening target value is set based on the amount of depressing the accelerator pedal signaled by the accelerator pedal position sensor, and the throttle actuator is driven to open and close the throttle valve to achieve the target value. The actual throttle valve opening is detected by the throttle opening sensor 1/2 attached to the throttle body. The throttle valve opening target value is compared with the throttle opening sensor 1 actual throttle opening value, and if the difference continues for more than the set value for more than the set time, the PGM-FI ECU determines that an error has occurred in the DBW system and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows: *: Throttle valve closed side **: Throttle valve open side D/C: Drive Cycle
Throttle valve opening target value and Actual Throttle Valve Opening Difference

items.text: Disconnection of cord (MTR2 line) between PGM-FI ECU and throttle actuator
Disconnection of code (MTR1 line) between PGM-FI ECU and throttle actuator

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
